name: cpu_raytrace
title: CPU ray traced rendering
short_description: Path traced render of a bundled scene on the processor.

structured_text.description: Renders a bundled 3D scene with a path tracer that runs entirely on the processor, tracing rays through a bounding volume hierarchy across all cores. The scene, sample count and resolution are fixed so the result depends only on the CPU.
structured_text.metric_explanation: The score is the number of completed render tiles per minute, reported as items per minute, which scales with how fast the full image is produced.
structured_text.value_interpretation: Higher is better. More tiles per minute means shorter waits for a finished frame and faster iteration on lighting and materials.
structured_text.reasoning: CPU path tracing is still the backbone of high quality offline rendering, and it stresses floating point math, memory access and scheduling together, which makes it a strong stand in for 3D production.

domain_gaming.explanation: Offline lightmap baking shares the same path tracing math, though play time uses the GPU.

domain_office.explanation: Office tasks never run ray traced rendering.

domain_software_development.explanation: Rendering is relevant only to developers building graphics tools.

domain_graphics_design.explanation: Product and packaging visuals are often rendered with a path tracer.

domain_video_editing.explanation: Rendered 3D elements are composited into edited footage.

domain_3d_rendering.explanation: This is the defining workload for offline 3D rendering and animation.

domain_audio_production.explanation: Audio work has no rendering component.

domain_cad_engineering.explanation: Engineering teams produce ray traced visualisations of their designs.

domain_scientific_computing.explanation: Scientific visualisation uses the same ray based rendering techniques.

domain_ai_training.explanation: Synthetic training images are sometimes generated with ray traced renders.

domain_ai_inference.explanation: Inference rarely depends on offline rendering.

domain_data_science.explanation: Analytics work does not involve ray tracing.

domain_server_virt.explanation: Render farms run this exact workload across many server nodes.
